entry.title: Re: SAP Sapphire 2026: The Autonomous Enterprise Has Arrived
entry.content: Great Insights

entry.title: Re: I want to modify the warehouse number that has alread...
entry.content:
Hello @SAPB1JIMI 


You cannot change an existing Warehouse Number once it is used in SAP.

try,
Create a new Warehouse Number
Reconfigure assignments
Transfer/migrate data (if needed)
Deactivate old warehouse (no deletion if used)

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: altSuperID is null
entry.content:
In SuccessFactors Learning ODATA API I am trying to obtain alternate supervisor IDs for a given user.

Sadly, despite the supervisors being set in the LMS, the altSuperID1, 2 and 3 fields are null instead of holding the userIDs.

SAP Support replied with a KB Article which says it's expected, because a user could have even up to 200 alternate managers and that the only way out is an enhancement request.

I wanted to ask if anyone has hit this obstacle in alt super IDs and worked around it?

--- Steps to Reproduce ---

  1. Go to LMS, search for a user, add an alternate manager to that user.
  2. Obtain LMS Token for Admin
  3. Call /learning/odatav4/user/v1/Users('userID')
  4. Observe all altSuperID are null. At least one of them should be populated as it is in the LMS.

entry.title: Automatic Account Determination in Actual Costing: Transaction Keys and General Modifications
entry.content:
This blog tries to answer the following questions in Actual Costing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

  • When CKMLCP posts closing journal entries, which G/L accounts are used and how are they determined?
  • What is the difference between selecting and unselecting "Revaluate Material" in the Post Closing step?
  • What are the PRY (and KDM) general modifications (PNL, PPL, PSL, PWP, empty), and what business process does each one represent?
    • Example: SG124_MA06 and FG126_MA06
  • How to configure Automatic Account Determination (SSCUI 100297) to post price differences from different processes to different G/L accounts?

Introduction

In my introductory blog on Actual Costing, I walked through a complete end-to-end example to show how price variances from raw material procurement and production are collected, rolled up, and allocated to COGS and ending inventory at period end. The Post Closing step in CKMLCP posts journal entries using transaction keys such as PRY, KDM, and BSX.

This blog goes deeper into those transaction keys, i.e. what each one means, how the "Revaluate Material" checkbox changes the posting behavior, and most importantly, how the General Modification feature of PRY and KDM lets you differentiate postings by process type. This is a powerful tool for you if you need granular P&L reporting on where price differences end up.

Transaction Keys Used in Actual Costing Closing Entries

When CKMLCP (Edit Actual Costing Runs) runs the Post Closing step, it posts journal entries using these transaction keys. You assign G/L accounts to them in SSCUI 100297 (Automatic Account Determination). The bold ones are specific to Actual Costing.

Transaction Key

What It Posts

BSX

Inventory account revaluation (stock at actual cost)

PRY

Price differences (the key Actual Costing transaction key)

KDM

Exchange rate differences on inventory

PRL

Activity price differences (from cost center to material)

GBB-AUI

Activity price difference credited to cost center

WPM

Price difference of material WIP build-up

PRM

Price difference of material WIP reduction

WPA

Price difference of activity WIP build-up

PRA

Price difference of activity WIP reduction

LKW

Accruals and deferrals (statistical, when not revaluating inventory)

COC

Revaluation of other consumption (when movement type group not in ML config)

UMB

Revenue/expense from revaluation (e.g., MR22, CKMLCP Post Closing)

In the introductory blog example, you could see PRY, KDM, BSX, and GBB all appearing in the same journal entry 4700000297. This blog explains how each of those postings is determined.

The "Revaluate Material" Checkbox — BSX vs. LKW

When you run the Post Closing step in CKMLCP, one of the key parameters is "Revaluate Material".

When selected (default behavior):
The system posts a real inventory revaluation. The inventory account (BSX) is debited. The price difference account (PRY) is credited.

  • Debit   BSX – Inventory account          +160.00 EUR  (stock revalued to actual cost)
  • Credit  PRY – Price differences          -160.00 EUR

When unselected:
The system uses a statistical posting instead. The inventory balance stays at standard price.

  • Debit   LKW – Accruals/Deferrals         +160.00 EUR   (statistical; no inventory impact)
  • Credit  PRY – Price differences          -160.00 EUR

The LKW option is used by customers who do not want to touch the inventory balance sheet account but still want price differences visible in P&L for analytical purposes.

General Modifications for PRY and KDM — Differentiate by Process

By default, all price differences land on the single G/L account assigned to PRY. Many customers want more analytical details:

  • How much of our price differences stayed in inventory vs. flowed to downstream products?
  • How much relates to WIP that we couldn't yet settle?
  • Which P&L account should capture differences from direct cost center consumption?

This is exactly what General Modification solves. By enabling it in SSCUI 100297, you can route each posting type to a different G/L account.

The Five General Modifications

General Modification

Label

Meaning

Posted on

PNL

Next Level

Price diff transferred to a higher-level material via multi-level rollup

The input (lower-level) material

PPL

Previous Level

Price difference received from a lower-level material

The output (higher-level) material

PSL

Single Level

Price diff from single-level consumption revaluation (cost centers, sales orders)

The consumed material

PWP

WIP

Price diff in WIP revaluation (open production orders at period end)

The material with open WIP

(empty)

Ending Inventory

Price diff remaining in ending inventory

The same material

Key relationship between PNL and PPL: They are always mirror entries for the same amount: one material "sends" via PNL, the other "receives" via PPL. Across all materials in scope, PNL and PPL net to zero.

The same five sub-modifications apply identically to KDM: KDM-PNL, KDM-PPL, KDM-PSL, KDM-PWP, KDM-empty.

You can refer to below configuration screenshots in SSCUI 100297, which is also used for our example in next section:

  • PRY with general modifications per valuation class:

Figure 1: PRY Configuration

 

  • KDM with general modifications shared by all valuation classes:

Figure 2: KDM Configuration

Example: SG124_MA06 and FG126_MA06

The following example uses data from SAP internal test system. SG124_MA06 is a semi-finished material (Plant 1010, Period 2/2026) consumed as input to produce three different finished goods:

Input Material

 

Output Material

Qty Consumed

SG124_MA06 (Semi-finished)

→

FG126_MA06

772 PC

SG124_MA06 (Semi-finished)

→

FG228_BB6_MA06

10 PC

SG124_MA06 (Semi-finished)

→

FG0612_MA06-1

10 PC

The following picture from Material Price Analysis (CKM3N) shows above consumption clearly:

Figure 3: SG124_MA06 Aggregated Consumption

 SG124_MA06 — Where Differences Originate

During the period, SG124_MA06 cumulates an inventory of 1485 PC with total price differences of 135.93 EUR (PRY) and exchange rate differences of 60.07 EUR (KDM).

After expanding folder of each produced product in CKM3N (Material Price Analysis) as above, under the Consumption section:

  • The cumulative quantity of 792 PC is broken down into individual finished goods
  • The PriceDiff column shows how the total price difference of 135.93 EUR (PRY) is distributed into finished goods (2.57, 70.70, 2.56 EUR respectively — all via PRY-PNL, G/L 52041500).
  • The ExRateDiff column shows how the total exchange rate difference of 60.07 EUR (KDM) is distributed into finished goods (0.40, 31.23, 0.41 EUR respectively — all via KDM-PNL, G/L 72020000)
  • The WIP row shows 1.73 EUR via PRY-PWP, and ending inventory shows 58.37 EUR via PRY-empty. 

Figure 4: SG124_MA06 Consumption Expanded

 

Destination

Quantity

PRY Share (EUR)

KDM Share (EUR)

Modification

Production → FG126_MA06

772 PC

70.70

31.23

PNL

Production → FG228_BB6_MA06

10 PC

2.56

0.41

PNL

Production → FG0612_MA06-1

10 PC

2.57

0.40

PNL

WIP (open production order 1012505)

20 PC

1.73

0.81

PWP

Ending inventory

673 PC

58.37

27.22

(empty)

Total cumulative

1485 PC

135.93

60.07

 

How This Looks in CKM3 — FG126_MA06

Figure 5: FG126_MA06

In CKM3N (Material Price Analysis) for FG126_MA06, the 70.70 EUR (PRY-PPL) and 31.23 EUR (KDM-PPL) from SG124_MA06 appear under Receipts → Production → SG124_MA06/1010. The 70.70 EUR on SG124_MA06 (PNL → G/L 52041500) and the 70.70 EUR on FG126_MA06 (PPL → G/L 52042000) are mirror entries for the same amount.

FI Journal Entry — Post Closing for SG124_MA06

The journal entry posted by CKMLCP (document 4700000297, ML Post Closing, 28.02.2026) contains the following key lines. Each uses a different G/L account from SSCUI 100297:

Figure 6: Closing Journal Entries from CKMLCP

 Journal Entry 4700000297: ML Post Closing — SG124_MA06 — Period 2/2026

Ln

G/L Account

Account Name

Key

Mod.

Debit (EUR)

Credit (EUR)

Remark

021

13300000

Inventory Semi Fin

BSX

 

85.59

 

Inventory revalued to actual

022

52543000

Gain Prc dif (PRY)

PRY

empty

 

216.13

Price diff — ending inventory

023

72510000

Gain exch.rate diff

KDM

empty

 

27.22

Exch.rate diff — ending inventory

024

52033000

Loss Val own prd

UMB

 

157.76

 

Revaluation offset

025

52041500

Loss Inv Val Adj Ext

PRY

PNL

 

70.70

SG124 sends to FG126 — Credit: diff leaves sender

026

72020000

Loss rnd.dif.f.curr

KDM

PNL

 

31.23

SG124 sends to FG126

027

52042000

Loss Prc dif (PRL)

PRY

PPL

70.70

 

FG126 receives — Debit: diff arrives at receiver

028

72040000

Loss curr.val.

KDM

PPL

31.23

 

FG126 receives

029

52041500

Loss Inv Val Adj Ext

PRY

PNL

 

2.56

SG124 sends to FG228

031

52042000

Loss Prc dif (PRL)

PRY

PPL

2.56

 

FG228 receives

033

52041500

Loss Inv Val Adj Ext

PRY

PNL

 

2.57

SG124 sends to FG0612

035

52042000

Loss Prc dif (PRL)

PRY

PPL

2.57

 

FG0612 receives

037

13200000

Inventory WIP

WPM

 

2.54

 

WIP build-up revalued

038

52046000

Loss Price diff fm l

PRY

PWP

 

1.73

WIP price diff — Credit: stays as WIP

039

72050100

Relz FX Loss NCsld

KDM

PWP

 

0.81

WIP exch.rate diff

The posting direction follows a consistent pattern: on the sending material (SG124_MA06), PRY-PNL is a Credit — the price difference

The posting direction follows a consistent pattern: on the sending material (SG124_MA06), PRY-PNL is a Credit — the price difference is "leaving" via multilevel rollup. On the receiving finished goods, PRY-PPL is a Debit — the same amount is "arriving". WIP (PWP) and ending inventory (empty) are also Credits on SG124, representing differences that stay rather than flow onwards.

How to Configure General Modifications in SSCUI 100297

  1. Open SSCUI 100297 — Automatic Account Determination
  2. Select area Sourcing and Procurement
  3. Specify transaction key PRY (or KDM), company code / chart of accounts
  4. In the Account Assignments section, click Edit Rules
  5. Enable the checkbox General Modification (optionally also Valuation Class for per-material-type differentiation)
  6. Add one row per sub-modification and assign your desired G/L account numbers

As shown in the SSCUI screenshots in the beginning, the example configuration has PRY with both General Modification and Valuation Class (3000 Raw Materials, 7900 Semifinished, 7920 Finished Goods), while KDM has no valuation class differentiation. 

Transaction Key

General Modification

Valuation Class

G/L Account

PRY

(empty)

3000 / 7900 / 7920

52043000

PRY

PNL

3000 / 7900 / 7920

52041500

PRY

PPL

3000 / 7900 / 7920

52042000

PRY

PSL

3000 / 7900 / 7920

52045000

PRY

PWP

3000 / 7900 / 7920

52046000

KDM

(empty)

—

72010000

KDM

PNL

—

72020000

KDM

PPL

—

72040000

KDM

PSL

—

72050000

KDM

PWP

—

72050100

Tip: If you don't need per-process differentiation, simply assign one account to PRY and one to KDM without any General Modification; the system uses them as a catch-all for all sub-modification types.

Why Does This Matter for Margin Analysis?

  1. Multi-level cost flow transparency: Of the total 135.93 EUR in SG124_MA06, exactly 75.83 EUR flowed to the three finished goods via account 52041500 (PNL). G/L 52042000 (PPL) on the receiving materials shows the same gross amount — enabling BOM cost tracing in your trial balance.
  2. WIP monitoring: Account 52046000 (PWP) isolates the 1.73 EUR locked in open production order 1012505 at period end. It will only be released when the order settles next period.
  3. PNL/PPL netting: The 70.70 EUR on SG124_MA06 (PNL, 52041500) and on FG126_MA06 (PPL, 52042000) net to zero across both materials — but on separate accounts, enabling gross BOM cost flow reporting.
  4. CO-PA integration: With General Modification, PSL on COGS uses G/L 52045000, enabling PA reports to distinguish "direct consumption variance" from "multilevel rollup variance" at product/customer level.

Summary

Topic

Key Points

Inventory revaluation account

BSX (real) or LKW (statistical): controlled by "Revaluate Material" checkbox

Price / exchange rate difference accounts

PRY for price diffs, KDM for exchange rate difference

Available general modifications

PNL (next level), PPL (from lower), PSL (single-level), PWP (WIP), empty (ending inventory)

PNL + PPL net to zero

Yes — always mirror entries for the same amount across the two materials

Where to configure

SSCUI 100297 → Edit Rules → Enable General Modification

Further differentiate by material type

Yes — additionally enable Valuation Class

General Modification mandatory

No — optional; without it all PRY/KDM use one catch-all account

Related Resources

  • Actual Costing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition — introductory blog
  • WIP Revaluation in SAP Actual Costing
  • Joint Production in Product Costing in SAP Cloud ERP
  • SAP Note 3617837: FAQ of Actual Costing in S/4HANA Cloud (Q14 covers account determination)

entry.title: Re: Does the WBS field on the sales order have an extension point?
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: SAP Convergent Charging 2022.2 updater becomes slow/u...
entry.content:
Hi Marius,

Thanks for contacting us.
The documents we normally require are listed in KBA 2667659 ("Data to retrieve for a first-level analysis of a SAP CC platform"). This includes the LOG and TRC files and, more importantly, the Garbage Collection log, the status of the cache (where the created subscriber data is held), and the thread dump files you mentioned (this is what we mostly need when troubleshooting performance).
Usually, it's not necessary to generate other reports directly from the JVM itself nor from the OS.
Since this is a considerable amount of sensitive data, it's best to file an actual customer ticket (you can reference this conversation there, for follow-up).

Based on what the documents show, we may have to ask you to adjust the trace settings (to get more thread dumps, or more traces from CC).
For now, as suggested by your message, there are numerous details to consider, so it's a bit difficult to address everything at once, especially before getting the usual info.
In the meantime, we can at least confirm that the way you use the updaters (active-passive mode, without any load balancer in the middle) is the proper way to go, for SAP CC.
Also, the audit feature alone is not expected to alter the performance, but it does rely on the database a lot. If the database is the bottleneck, the thread dumps should show it.

Another important point is the recommend initial tuning: can you please make sure that your CC system already complies with KBA 2006073 ("JVM option recommendations, up to SAP CC 2022")?
This KBA is supposed to be sufficient for productive systems, but different settings can be considered based on the context (for example if you temporarily need to improve the updater's performance during a heavy provisioning cycle).

Best regards.

François
SAP Convergent Charging Support

entry.title: where exactly i can see the Define Number Ranges for Operations
entry.content:
On which table or tcode i can see this Number range and its document

checked in process order and table AFVC. not able to get this

entry.title: Export BW4 to BDC
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm trying to calculate the cost of exporting BW4 (full and then delta) to Business Data Cloud.

I know the volume, the decompression factor, and the cost per CU, but I can't calculate the cost of my export in CUs.

Does anyone know how I can calculate the CU consumption rate for a full data export from SAP BW/4HANA via BDC?

Thank you

entry.title: Document Ai Service
entry.content:
Hi

I am using the document Ai feature for invoices,  I need to get the purchase order number and the document ai service works great when there is a label for the field e.g. PO or Purchase order.

We have certain invoices that will have the purchase order number added in a text section with no title, in this case the po number is not returned, 

I am working with the json data coming back from Document Ai Premium Service but it does not return any reference to the po number as it is not labelled.  

Is there anyway to extract this data ?

Many thanks 

Darren

entry.title: Re: SAP Flexible Workflow Custom Scenario development with My Inbox Integration
entry.content:
Thank you I found it very helpful.
Just an update to people who are using this blog and stuck at getting the Required Action drop down to show the value "To Requestor".. In S4HANA 2023, it was only visible once I set the flag in Activities tab as "Can only be used for handling Exception".

Regards

entry.title: Could be eslint-plugin-fiori-custom updated to newer version?
entry.content:
Hi,

I am using a eslint-plugin-fiori-custom package in my project and I recently got an issue "Outdated open-source components are consumed in following projects within your responsibility area. A component is outdated if it is older than 12 months independent of newer versions being available or not."

As the package was not updated for 3 years, I cannot update the version in my project  and my only option is to remove it. The package is not vital for our projects but it is useful for our development and simply refreshing the component version would allow me to continue using it. Thanks!

Jakub

entry.title: Re: Managing JMS Queues Programmatically with the new SAP Cloud Integration JMS OData API
entry.content:
@upputholla 
Can you please elaborate on this : "customers implementing time-window slicing combined with scheduled jobs to handle high-volume queues reliably".   What does the issue you are facing, and what does the time-window slicing mean here.
Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Trial Cloud Identity Services: first administrator ne...
entry.content: Hello , I have received below response from SAP today "hank you for getting back to us. We would like to inform you that this is a known bug currently limiting the functionality of Cloud Identity Services. Our team is actively working on a fix. At this point, we do not yet have a confirmed resolution date. We will keep users updated as soon as more information becomes available. We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience this may cause and appreciate your patience and understanding."

entry.title: Re: SAP FSM 2605 – Finally! Stop Technicians from Closing Smartforms with Incomplete Data
entry.content: Very Good! update. Thanks Sakil.

entry.title: Re: What is your opinion on Document AI - line item capab...
entry.content:
I don't have access to creating incidents, but actually this was also just an example. I have seen plenty of other examples of strange behaviour.

I was more interested in getting a general understanding of the quality of SAP Document AI, so I was hoping to hear about other users experience - good and bad - but it seems nobody want to share their experience.

entry.title: Re: TST03 Table space issue
entry.content:
Hi @SAPTest 

SAP Community - Collected information about reclaim / shrink / defragmentation topic in context of SAP HANA persistence (with example)

Regards,
Laszlo

entry.title: Re: SAC Comment widget date filter
entry.content:
Hi Samuel,

The only solution I’ve found is as follows:

  1. Set up a page filter using the custom date from your story.
  2. Link the page filter to the comment widget only.

The comment widget should then display only the comments for the selected date whenever you change the story filter.

Best regards,

Matthias

entry.title: Unable to Assign House Bank Account ID (GIRO) in S/4HANA Fiori
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I created a bank with Bank Key 33333352 and House Bank D-B52 in my demo system using Company Code GR01. I am now trying to assign the House Bank Account ID GIRO in the Manage Bank Accounts Fiori app, as required in my exercise. However, whenever I enter the value, the system displays an "Invalid Entry" message. I have already created the bank and house bank successfully, but I am unable to proceed with the account ID.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Balance Sheet/Income Statement - Multidimensional - N...
entry.content:
Hi,

I was able to generate the report by applying the filter "Financial Statement Leaf Item (Node)", as shown in the screenshot below.

Using this filter, the report displays only the required P&L nodes and excludes the unwanted Balance Sheet accounts that were appearing under the "Not Assigned" section.

Additionally, with the P&L - Actuals app, I am able to analyze the results by different time periods such as monthly, quarterly, and yearly, which fulfills the reporting requirement.  there also "Node" filter option available. 

 

Thanks,

silambarasan R

entry.title: Re: How To Trigger Automated Email Notifications Based on KPI Thresholds in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Smart workaround using scripting!
It's so so strange to me -and hard to realize- that this common requirement is still not possible with the SAC standard feature set, even with the feature names "Data Change insights" and now "Watchlist" 😅 😅

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA on Premise – Adding custom new Pre-Conditions with F4 Value help in Flexible Workflo
entry.content: Thanks a lot for sharing. The post is very helpful!

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 02 - Content for common reuse types

Hi Experts,

 

I would like to know how to create a Planned Order through MRP with Special Procurement type "3" subcontracting. (PLAF-SOBES = "3").

entry.title: Re: Automatic Warehouse Task Creation at GR Flag not working
entry.content: But the Settings is given at Warehouse Process Type level so it should work while Posting Goods Receiept in EWM too right?

entry.title: Re: New SAP Community - Profile Signatures
entry.content:
@thomas_jung Thank you very much for your update! I am now able to use the signature generator.

However, I am still not seeing my signature or other users signatures 😶 

Since setting up the signature I have posted a blog (IAM App & Successor in Same Business Role? Don’t I... - SAP Community) as well as answers (Unable to Restrict Edit Functionality in Display M... - SAP Community) but none of them include a signature.

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 03 - Declarative field control with @mandatory

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: Data Tiering Strategy and Best Practices
entry.content:
Thank you for the blog. Your blogs are always helful. I just have a question, as we understand that the Merge function in the object store space is compute intensive, do you still think that the option - 

5.2 Pattern 2 – Cold‑First Datasphere Object Store, Selectively Promoted → Warm/Hot is efficient way to follow, as there would be frequent updates in the recent data, the merge task may be compute intensive.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
OData vs SOAP for .NET WMS Integration in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I am seeking technical advice regarding the integration of a .NET-based WMS solution developed by our team with  SAP Business ByDesign.

As we move away from historical on-premise setups toward a decoupled 'Side-by-Side' architecture, we want to use the app with SAP Public Cloud . I have two critical questions for those who have managed similar cloud transitions:

1. API Utilization for .NET Integration
Since our WMS middleware is built on .NET, we need to ensure robust communication with the SAP core.

  • Which specific OData services or SOAP APIs are recommended for real-time warehouse tasks (e.g., Goods Receipt, Stock Transfer, Picking)in SAP Public Cloud ?
  • For those using SAP Public Cloud, are you primarily using the SiteLogisticsTask or WarehouseConfirmation web services, and how do they perform under the high-frequency request load typical of a .NET scanning application?

2. Public Cloud WM Capability & Responsiveness
We are evaluating how the Warehouse Management (WM) module in the Public Cloud responds to external 'exploits' or calls from a third-party .NET interface.

  • Does the Public Cloud WM module offer the same level of flexibility as the historical on-premise WM/EWM when it comes to API-triggered movements?
  • Are there known performance limitations or 'throttling' issues when a .NET application performs high-volume scanning directly against the Public Cloud tenant?
  • In your experience, does the Public Cloud WM architecture provide sufficient coverage for complex scenarios when managed via external APIs?

We want to ensure that our project respects the 'Clean Core' strategy while maintaining the historical reliability that  our company has built into the  App over the years.

I would greatly appreciate any technical documentation, API maps, or feedback from your own cloud WMS implementations.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
Re: Under the Hood of ABAP CDC in SAP Datasphere Replication Flows
entry.content:
Hello @anilkumarauce 

thank you very much for this great and detailed Blog!

Especially your picture is very helpful.

However one question remains, for me: What happens specifically, when you pause the replication flow in Datasphere? Do you know, if the S4 system is somehow "informed" about it, or is it just a Datasphere internal "switch" to delay the replication until it is resumed?

Thanks and BR,

Martin

entry.title: Re: Restriction to avoid printing parked receipts, even m...
entry.content:
Hi Jhon,

You can exclude printing parked receipts in the print template configuration.
Each template has additional options. For the sales receipt template, there is an option called "Print parked receipts." Once you add this option and set it to false, printing of parked receipts will be disabled.

Best regards,
Martin

entry.title: Re: Introduction to the Parallel Line
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Greetings!!

We currently have the following systems in our landscape:

  • Development
  • Test
  • Test Parallel
  • Production

We would like to refresh the Production data into the Test system to ensure proper testing.

Could you please suggest the best approach for this activity? Also, please confirm whether this requires any separate/additional service and if there will be any associated charges.

Thanks

A.Bhaskar

entry.title: Unlock the Power of Procurement
entry.content:
The “Discovering Procurement Solutions” course on SAP Learning is your gateway to understanding how modern businesses manage purchasing smarter and faster.

This course is built with you in mind—clear, engaging, and easy to follow. Whether you're new to procurement or expanding your knowledge, you’ll gain practical insights you can actually use.

👉 Learn how procurement drives business value
👉 Explore SAP solutions in real-world scenarios
👉 Build confidence with step-by-step, flexible learning

Don’t just keep up—get ahead. Join the Discovering Procurement Solutions course and take the next step in your learning journey.

Learn. Grow. Succeed

entry.title: SmartRecruiters Outlook Integration
entry.content:
Hi,
do you know how to integrate SmartRecruiters to Outlook?
Does Outlook integration require setup in SuccessFactors platform (Security Center, Work Tech Configuration) or in administration settings directly in SmartRecruiters or  both?

Regards

entry.title: Re: Seeking Feedback on My SAP CAP/BTP Learning Roadmap (student)
entry.content: Your learning roadmap looks solid, especially the way you’ve structured CAP → BTP → real project practice. One suggestion: add a small “build‑and‑deploy” cycle early in the journey. Even a simple CAP service deployed to BTP gives huge confidence and helps connect all the concepts. Keep going — you’re on the right track.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2025 Fully-Activated Appliance: Known Issues
entry.content:
Hi greg_not_so,

DDIC should be able to unlock other users and also assign additional authorizations if needed.

What do you mean by „immovable“ ?

Best Joerg

entry.title: Re: ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code is now available on the VS Code marketplace!
entry.content:
@CRDESHPANDE @Evaristo_EBC @nitesh0506 @dukejib5 This is all because only SNC with SSO destinations are supported in the first version. Support for all kinds of RFC destinations (non-SNC, password) is planned for the next release.

@CRDESHPANDE Changing the port like this will not work. Port 48xx is RFC with SNC. Port 33xx is RFC without SNC.

entry.title: Re: AI across Customer Project processes
entry.content: Great breakdown of how AI can streamline customer project processes. What stands out to me is the shift from isolated automation to connected, context‑aware workflows across BTP. The real value shows up when AI is embedded directly into project governance, not just task execution. Looking forward to seeing more examples of end‑to‑end orchestration in real customer landscapes.

entry.title: Re: FINAL PAYGUIDE FIELDS NOT UPDATING WHEN A PROMOTION T...
entry.content: Hello David! Thanks for responding and yes, i got to this conclusion yesterday while testing and reading documentation again. Thank you very much for the confirmation. Just to explore all possibilities, do you think there is a way to perform this update without saving? Only way i can think would have payguides in a lookup table, or something like that... gois custom instead of standard. But maybe is just better to keep the std way. Thank you again!

Dear Ashwin, 

Please refer to the note 2268116.

BR, Zhiger

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 04 - Namespaces, contexts and name prefixes

@Noodl

Yes, programs are planned for the next release. Please see the 'What comes next?' section above in the blog post.

Embedded SAP GUI support is currently not planned.

entry.title: Re: Working with Git commands
entry.content: Nice walkthrough of the essential Git commands. What I really like here is the focus on practical, day‑to‑day usage rather than theory. For anyone working on CAP or BTP projects, mastering these basics early makes collaboration and CI/CD pipelines much smoother. Good refresher for the community.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions

entry.title: Re: How to Prevent Duplicate GST Numbers in Customer and...
entry.content:
Hi @SivaNagiReddy ,

Yes, if you maintain the GST Number in the Identification tab using the tax number category IN3 (GST Registration Number), SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a standard option to perform duplicate GST number validation.

Please refer to the following KBAs for detailed information:

KBA 2886187 – How to Activate Tax Number Category Duplication Check for Business Partners in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

KBA 2886241 – How to Change Duplicate Tax Number Category from an Information Message to an Error Message in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

You can use the following SSCUIs:

  • SSCUI 102415 – Maintain Tax Number Categories: To activate duplicate checks for GST numbers.

  • SSCUI 103601 – Change Message Control for Duplicate Tax Numbers: To change the duplicate tax number message from a warning/information message to an error message.

These configurations will help prevent the creation of Business Partners with duplicate GST numbers.

Regards,
Vinith

entry.title: Re: Handling Large Volumes of Data Between SAP CPI and S/4 HANA
entry.content: valuable tips.. Thanks 👍

entry.title: Re: Does the WBS field on the sales order have an extensi...
entry.content:

  • Try Customer Influence Portal

entry.title: Configuration for Automatic HU Packing during GR for Production Orders
entry.content:
Hi Team,

For finished products, HU packing during Goods Receipt with respect to the Production Order is expected to happen automatically.

Could you please provide the relevant configuration details in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that control automatic HU packing for this scenario?

Thanks & Regards,
Kiran

entry.title: SAP Fiori Reporting only
entry.content: Implementing SAP Fiori Reporting only, without deploying the broader SAP Fiori application suite - what are the challenges one should be aware of.

entry.title: Re: Behavior of Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation after Clearing
entry.content:
HI dear @KevinSigl 

Thank you for the clarification regarding the statistical posting behavior in Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation.

You mentioned that you have already started analyzing possible scenarios to avoid the statistical posting. Could you please share any updates or findings on this topic?

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Expense and VAT Posting Issue for Credit Ca...
entry.content:
I would avoid posting both documents as if they are two separate purchases.

In this case the card transaction and the supplier tax invoice are evidence for the same underlying spend. If Concur already posted the expense and liability, then creating a normal supplier invoice later can easily double book the expense and input VAT.

I would add a matching step before any supplier invoice is posted from the tax invoice:

match by employee/card holder, supplier, transaction date, amount, currency and tax invoice number.

If there is already a Concur/card entry for the same spend, the tax invoice should either be attached to that existing expense record or handled through a clearing/reclassification process, not booked as a new expense.

If the supplier tax invoice amount, VAT amount, supplier name or date does not match the card transaction cleanly, route it to AP review. But if it does match, the process should preserve the tax invoice for compliance without creating a second expense or duplicate input VAT claim

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
Re: Blocked bin don´t permit transfer
entry.content: Hi Chris, have a nice day. The client wants the blocked position to allow only material transfer and not material removal.

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting Planned Order Creation Inquiry
entry.content: Maintain procurement type + special procurement in the material master, run MRP, and convert the planned order. This ensures PLAF-SOBES = 3 is populated and the subcontracting flow works correctly.

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: From Business Intent to Enterprise-Ready AI Agents
entry.content: As an SAP partner we are already using Joule Studio 2.0, should we do something to get Joule Studio 2.0 design-time access?

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
Re: MR21 – New Price Field Greyed Out – Standard Price No...
entry.content:
The MR21 transaction for updating the new standard price is greyed out in a specific plant.

Check Material Ledger Activation

  • Confirm whether the Material Ledger (ML) is active in the plant.
  • Execute SPRO → CKMSTART to activate the Material Ledger if required.

Review Error Messages

  • Check for any system-generated error messages (e.g., CKPRCH 038 indicating missing authorization).

Else provide some information on those materials (type of material, etc.)

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
CBC Mandatory SD Organizational Units Due to Baseline Accelerator Scope Selection
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are working on an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3SL) implementation.

During project scoping, the Baseline Accelerator was used, which automatically included several SD-related scope items. However, the customer does not have any SD business processes in scope and does not plan to use SD functionality.

As a result, CBC now requires us to create several SD organizational units (Sales Organization, Distribution Channel, Division, Shipping Points, and related assignments) to complete the Organizational Structure activity.

We attempted to remove the related SD scope items, but CBC no longer allows us to deselect them.

Would there be any functional, technical, or maintenance concerns if we simply create these mandatory organizational units to complete CBC, while leaving them otherwise unused?

Additionally, is there a recommended SAP approach for handling scope items that were automatically selected by the Baseline Accelerator but are not required by the customer?

Thank you in advance for your support.

BR,
Evrim

@Melis_Sorgun 

@MerveBayturk

entry.title: WorkforcePerson Integration from SF to S4 HANA via MDI
entry.content:
We have a Requirement to Store the "Position" in S4 HANA Public Cloud from SF through MDI. In SuccessFactors we have the standard Field called  "Position" and same receiving to MDI. 

SAP GROW don't have any standard "Position" and Created a Custom Field and we are Implemented  the BADI WFD_MDI_EXT_FIELD_MAPPING, its working , whereas our actual Requirement is Standard Read the Field of MDI.

Can somebody help us how to proceed with this requirement?

entry.title: PoC Real in CNE5 w/o values PS
entry.content: What is the exact condition that triggers CNE1 to generate actual POC records? Is there a specific configuration or prerequisite that enables actual POC calculation beyond what is described above?

entry.title: Re: AI across Customer Project processes
entry.content: @Kapil_Kumar01 Thank you for your question. While i am not an integration expert to answer your question, but the perspective of viewing things change when it comes to AI & agents... One could for example use an agent to extract information of the lead and create a project on the basis all the while not having the integration in place..provided the APIs and data is in place..Agents can orchestrate the process and run these steps..

entry.title: Re: Under the Hood of ABAP CDC in SAP Datasphere Replication Flows
entry.content:
Hi @MKreitlein ,

When you pause a replication flow, the S/4HANA system still continue to capture the delta changes and push the changes to the buffer table so that the Datasphere can pull all that delta information from Buffer table once we resume the Replication flow. Post that the processed data will be cleared from the buffer table.

Regards,

Anil Kumar.

entry.title: Re: Your S/4HANA & Ariba implementation is live, but your GRC ruleset do not have Ariba rules co
entry.content:
Dear Madhu,

 

We are in Ariba implementation, plan is use existing GRC 12.0 with extension to  Ariba . 

Now client requirement is we need to control Ariba groups in Purchasing Groups,P,Org,Relse code,Section code level, Plant level,etc..

And also activity level control also they are expecting for ariba groups.

We know, we cannot trim Ariba groups into granule level.

Please suggest if you come across this in your Ariba implementation projects.

entry.title: Meet the AI Layer in SAP Solution Scout
entry.content:
SAP Solution Scout lets you navigate the SAP portfolio by industry value chain—from Business Priorities down to individual Solution Capabilities—and the capability detail view now includes the supporting AI offerings that come with each capability. This means that when you evaluate a capability, you see not only the value proposition, value drivers, and related innovations, but also which AI Assistants and AI Agents are embedded. That’s how Enterprise AI delivers real value—not as a roadmap, but as deployed capability.
This integrated view changes the exploration question from "does this capability cover my requirement?" to "does this capability cover my requirement, and how intelligent is it?"
SAP Solution Scout is self-service and available to SAP customers, partners, and employees—explore at your own pace and build a collection of relevant capabilities and AI offerings. Start exploring at https://solutionscout.sap.com.

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM MFS - Short Dump while starting the communication channel.
entry.content: thank you so much its working fine

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud, HANA Database: Jump start mission - Database connection command "Knowledge Check"
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

Recently I have completed a course (Mission) from SAP Learning  and done all the steps mentioned there with succesfully passed the "Knowledge Check" Options. The learning pathe is as below. 

 

Jump Start Your SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database > Create a Development Project and Calculation View >
Grant Access to Calculation Views
 

But at the "Knowledge check" of the last section, I have answered the question correctly but it shows the error as below in screenshot. The question was

"Enter the SQL command used to log in as a different user in the SAP HANA Database Explorer." 

I have answered with the command used to connect to the DB "CONNECT report PASSWORD Password1;". But the apps are showing the error "Sorry, that’s not quite right.". 

Need your cooperation to resolve the problem.

 

 

Thanks

Wahid

entry.title: Sales Analysis Report – Incorrect Gross Profit Calculation for Service Items Linked to Resource
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

We are facing an issue in SAP Business One HANA version 10 where the Gross Profit in Sales Analysis Report is showing incorrect values. In this sales order,  Service Items are linked with Resource Master Data.

Kindly let us know how gross profit calculates here. what is formula. 

we renamed menu sales order to work order as per customer requirement. 

In General setting, Gross Profit on selling Price made it. 

Kindly advise how gross profit calculates. here. 

We have attached the screenshot for reference.

 

Thans

entry.title: Re: MR21 – New Price Field Greyed Out – Standard Price No...
entry.content: Check whether Accounting view has been extended to those materials

entry.title: Estratégia de liberação para Requisição originada de serviços de PM
entry.content: Todas as configurações feitas por analista funcional do módulo de Materiais no SAP ECC foram feitas, SPRO, CT04. CL02, CL20N, CL24N, para estratégia liberação para Requisições de compras originadas de ordem de manutenção criadas NA  transação IW31 e após liberação na transação IW32 para Serviços, estes criados na transação AC01 - SAP ECC,  quando liberada na transação IW32 não é gerada ou ativada para a estratégia de liberação Configura.  Feito os testes diretamante na  transação ME51N para a mesma ordem de PM e gera a estratégia.   O que pode ser isso ? Lembrando que ainda não estou envolvendo o ABAP. Apliquei a tabela chave CEBAN e todos os principais campos, tipo de Requisição, quantidade, valor, tipos de classificação de item,  etc, porem não está levando para liberação.  Algumas orientações pediram para fazer com ABAP via BAPI ou aplicar método.  Agradeço sua atenção.

Take the First Step Toward a Connected Enterprise

Understanding business networks is no longer optional—it’s essential for navigating today’s digital economy. By completing this course, you’ll gain valuable insights into how organizations collaborate smarter and operate more efficiently.

🚀Start your learning journey today: Discovering Business Network Solutions 

Join the Conversation

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC - Electronic Invoicing for Italy : ItalyReceiveNotification without SNI
entry.content: Thanks. So you have the SAP-supported E2E process if you are also using SAP custom domain. Where does SNI then come into the picture? DRC Italy allows you to connect directly with the SDI platform without needing another third party.

entry.title: Re: ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code is now available on the VS Code marketplace!
entry.content:
I used Claude to extract this extension and manually added my on‑premise system to test it. It worked, but it doesn’t provide any methods or features to create Function Modules, reports, or database tables. It still basically works like ADT for ABAP Cloud.

At least there’s now an official MCP for ABAP, so I don’t need to use other MCPs from GitHub.

entry.title: Re: Plant language change from EN to JA ( Japanese) for JP00
entry.content:
Recommended Procedure for Language Change in Plant Configuration:

  1. Review SAP Note/KBA 3665722

    • "Unable to change the language of a plant in configuration 'Define Plant'"

  2. Execute Configuration Steps

    • Transaction: OX10
    • SSCUI: 105877
    • Navigate to Address/Communication Data to modify the plant language.

  3. Validate Customized Forms & Messages

    • Ensure all developed/customized forms and messages support the new language setting.
    • Check also for master data long text language 

Note: Failure to update dependent objects may result in inconsistencies.

entry.title: Variable Pay: how to correctly configure field formats for Business Goals
entry.content:
Hi,

I have configured business goals, but the percentage for Goal Section Weight still displays 600% instead of 60%

the business Section Amount should be 5310 EUR, instead of 53103.78

for business goals the amount should be divided by 10 and rounded up.

for example, the value: 15931,13 / 10 = 1593 EUR

can you please give me an advice how to set it up?

I tried several formats in Set Number Format Rules and than used them for specific field in Set Number Formats, but I don´t seem to get it right.
thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: Behind the Curtains - what's new about Maintenance Certificates
entry.content:

*** ERROR => DlLoadLib()==DLENOACCESS - dlopen("/usr/sap//SYS/exe/run/libsapcrypto.so") FAILED
"/usr/sap//SYS/exe/run/libsapcrypto.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory" [dlux_mt.c 445]
*** ERROR => SapSecuInit: could not load libsapsecu >/usr/sap//SYS/exe/run/libsapcrypto.so< [secuload.c 554]

Is there a solution?

entry.title: PS certifications Public edition

Hello Gurus

Can you help me with the list of certification codes or resources for PS (Project System) on S4 HANA Public Cloud edition?

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud Activation on SAP BTP and Database Operations Using JDBC from Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Introduction

While working on integration scenarios in SAP BTP, there are many cases where SAP Cloud Integration needs to directly read from or write to a database. SAP HANA Cloud is the obvious choice on BTP, and the JDBC adapter in Cloud Integration makes this integration straightforward—once the setup is clear.

In this blog, I will walk through:

  • How to activate SAP HANA Cloud in SAP BTP
  • How to create a simple database structure (schema, table, user)
  • How to connect SAP Cloud Integration to HANA Cloud using JDBC
  • How to perform basic SELECT, INSERT, and UPDATE operations from an iFlow

The focus of this blog is mainly on the enablement and configuration steps, because that is where most issues usually occur.

You can read my previous blogs here on SAP TMS and SAP ANS Integration with Cloud Integration

Content Transport Using SAP Cloud Transport Management Service for SAP CI CF Environment

Setting Up Alerts for SAP Cloud Integration Using SAP Alert Notification Service 

 

Prerequisites

Before starting, make sure you have:

  • Access to an SAP BTP Global Account
  • A Subaccount (Cloud Foundry environment)
  • SAP Integration Suite enabled
  • Required roles to:
    • Create HANA Cloud instances
    • Access HANA Database Explorer
    • Configure Cloud Integration

Step 1: Enable SAP HANA Cloud in SAP BTP

Check Entitlements

First, ensure SAP HANA Cloud is entitled:

  1. Go to BTP Cockpit ->Global Account ->Entitlements

Check if SAP HANA Cloud is available and assign quota to your subaccount if needed

If the entitlement is missing, you won’t be able to create a HANA Cloud instance.

Subscribe SAP HANA Cloud Tools application

  1. Navigate to Subaccount -> Instances and Subscriptions ->Create

Select Service: SAP HANA Cloud and Plan: tools

 

Create SAP HANA Cloud Instance

  1. Navigate to Subaccount ->Instances and Subscriptions->Create

Select Service: SAP HANA Cloud and Plan: hana
Provide Instance name (example: hana-cloud-ci-db) and System password

Create the instance , provisioning usually takes 20–30 minutes, so be patient here.

Verify Database Status

Once the instance is created:

Assign below roles to your user to open and perform operations in HANA Cloud 

Navigate to Subaccount ->Security->Users ->Assign Roles

  1. Open SAP HANA Cloud from the subaccount 
Make sure the database status is Running

Step 2: Create Database Objects

Open HANA Database Explorer

  1. From HANA Cloud Central->Open Database Explorer
Log in using the admin user (DBADMIN) 
\
Select SQL Console

Create Schema and Table

Create a schema first:

CREATE SCHEMA CI_DEMO_SCHEMA;

Now create a simple table:

CREATE TABLE CI_DEMO_SCHEMA.EMPLOYEE (

    EMP_ID INTEGER NOT NULL,

    EMP_NAME NVARCHAR(100),

    DEPARTMENT NVARCHAR(50),

    SALARY DECIMAL(15, 2),

    PRIMARY KEY (EMP_ID)

);

 

Insert some test data:

INSERT INTO CI_DEMO_SCHEMA.EMPLOYEE

VALUES (1, 'ABC DEF', 'IT', 90000);

 

Create a Technical User for Integration
Do not use DBADMIN for integration access.

CREATE USER CI_JDBC_USER PASSWORD "Pas****@1**3";
GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE ON SCHEMA CI_DEMO_SCHEMA TO CI_JDBC_USER;

This user will be used later in Cloud Integration.

Step 3: Configure JDBC Connectivity in Cloud Integration

Get JDBC Connection Details

From HANA Cloud Central, note the host by Copy SQL Endpoint

98dd5c42*****************0d8792.hna1.prod-us10.hanacloud.ondemand.com:443

HANA Cloud JDBC URL format required  as per below :

jdbc:sap://<host>:443/?encrypt=true and Port 443 is used with SSL.

The complete URL will be become as per below :

jdbc:sap://98dd5c42*****************0d8792.hna1.prod-us10.hanacloud.ondemand.com:443/?encrypt=true

Navigate to Integration Suite->Security ->Manage JDBC Data Source and maintain as per below 

Configure JDBC Adapter in iFlow

HTTPS Sender to Trigger message from Postman and JDBC Receiver 

In JDBC connection maintain the JDBC Data Source name created in previous step.

Step 4: Testing

1. Select Operation 

Configure the Postman to trigger CI iflow to trigger the HTTP endpoint

SELECT * FROM CI_DEMO_SCHEMA.EMPLOYEE;
 

2. Insert Operation

<root>
    <insert_statement>
        <dbTableName action="INSERT">
            <table>CI_DEMO_SCHEMA.EMPLOYEE</table>
            <access>
                <EMP_ID>2</EMP_ID>
                <EMP_NAME>GGG HHHH</EMP_NAME>
                <DEPARTMENT>SAP</DEPARTMENT>
                <SALARY>99000.00</SALARY>
            </access>
        </dbTableName>
    </insert_statement>
</root>

 

Perform select again to view the inserted record 

3. Update operation :

<root>
    <update_statement>
        <dbTableName action="UPDATE">
            <table>CI_DEMO_SCHEMA.EMPLOYEE</table>
            <access>
                <EMP_NAME>NNN JJJ</EMP_NAME>
            </access>
            <key>
                <EMP_ID>2</EMP_ID>
            </key>
        </dbTableName>
    </update_statement>
</root>


Perform select again to view updated record 
Table from HABA Cloud DB 

Summary 

This POC highlighted several common pitfalls when working with SAP HANA Cloud and the JDBC adapter in SAP Cloud Integration. Most issues were not related to connectivity, but rather to user security policies, JDBC execution modes, and strict XML structure requirements. Understanding these early helps avoid hours of debugging.

Reference 

JDBC Receiver Adapter Payload Operations

SAP HANA Cloud

entry.title: Re: Blocked bin don´t permit transfer
entry.content:
Hi Carlos,

In standard EWM blocking a bin for putaway means that no materials can be put into this storage bin.
While block for stock removal means that no materials can be removed from this storage bin.

Regards,
João

entry.title: Re: Why SAP S/4HANA Transformations Fail When Business Processes Are Automated Before They Are Optim
entry.content: Great insights and perspective.

entry.title: Re: ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code is now available on the VS Code marketplace!

entry.title: How to speed up blog post review?
entry.content:
Hello, colleagues.

I've created blog post few days ago but it's still in "Awaiting Review" status. Do I need to ping someone directly?

entry.title: How Can You Position SAP Business Network Solutions with Confidence?
entry.content:
The “Positioning SAP Solutions for Business Network Solutions for Partners” course is designed to help you do exactly that—build confidence, sharpen your messaging, and understand where SAP Business Network fits in real-world scenarios.

 

Knowing how to position SAP Business Network solutions helps you connect these needs to the right offerings—and that’s where this course comes in. 
This course moves beyond basics and focuses on real-world positioning skills for partners.

You’ll explore:

  • How to identify customer needs and business challenges
  • Where SAP Business Network solutions fit into different scenarios
  • How to articulate value clearly and effectively
  • Key messaging to position solutions confidently in conversations

It’s practical, relevant, and directly applicable to your day-to-day work.

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • SAP partners and consultants
  • Pre-sales and sales professionals
  • Anyone involved in solution positioning and customer engagement

If your role involves explaining or recommending SAP solutions—this course will elevate your impact.

You can start today and immediately apply what you learn

Join the discussion

How do you position SAP Business Network solutions in your projects or conversations? Share your insights or questions in the comments—we’d love to hear from you!

entry.title: Re: Procurement of Services in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Latest Guidance on Services Procurement Solutions

For latest guidance on services procurement solutions, kindly refer page below.

  • What is services procurement?
